human_verification.notes: Reviewed the construction-and-field-services field-reporting package for reporting-boundary quality, authority-source quality, metric-definition handling, source-lineage discipline, permit and inspection gate coverage, closeout blocker handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to construction and field-services reporting support, source tie-outs, metric-definition handling, disclosure caveats, and escalation for permit, inspection, safety, closeout, and licensed-authority conflicts.

Authority sources cover OSHA recordkeeping, OSHA construction safety, official permit and inspection guidance, official daily QC reporting artifacts, public closeout workflow references, and recognized construction-management boundary references with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover disputed metrics, stale or conflicting records, incomplete closeout inputs, permit-readiness gaps, inspection blockers, unsupported claims, code-boundary refusals, safety-changed-condition escalations, adjacent-specialist routing, and tenant-specific adaptations.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/construction-and-field-services/field-reporting-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated construction and field-services reporting overlay where permit, inspection, legal, licensed-trade, design, and final-approval authority remain external.
The specialist is intentionally collaboration-oriented and returns to the orchestrator whenever missing prerequisites, source conflicts, or authority shifts prevent safe reporting.
